active.name: Push Day

blocks.title: Block 1 — Bench press (straight sets)
blocks.note: 3 straight sets — descend weight, chase near-failure each set

exercises.name: Flat or incline DB bench press
exercises.note: Pick flat or incline each session. Push to near-failure on each set.
exercises.tip: Full ROM. Touch chest, lock out at top. Control the descent — 2 seconds down.

blocks.title: Block 2 — Shoulder circuit
blocks.note: 2 rounds — move exercise to exercise, rest at end of round

exercises.name: Seated DB overhead press
exercises.tip: Seated on bench, core braced. Press to full lockout, lower controlled.

exercises.name: DB lateral raise
exercises.tip: Lead with elbows, slight forward lean. Pause briefly at the top.

exercises.name: Plate circles
exercises.tip: Arms extended, draw big slow circles. Keep shoulders packed down.

blocks.title: Block 3 — Push finisher circuit
blocks.note: 2 rounds — higher rep, lower load, finish strong

exercises.name: Push-up variation
exercises.note: Rotate: wide grip / close grip / decline / archer
exercises.tip: Pick a variation that challenges you. Stop 1–2 reps before form breaks.

exercises.name: DB chest fly
exercises.tip: Slight bend in elbows throughout. Feel the stretch at the bottom, squeeze at top.

exercises.name: DB skull crusher
exercises.tip: Upper arms vertical, lower to temples. Slow and controlled.

active.progression: On Block 1, when you can hit the top of each rep range with clean form, bump the starting weight up 5 lbs next session. On circuits, add 1 rep per set before adding weight.

active.name: Pull Day

blocks.title: Block 1 — Row (straight sets)
blocks.note: 3 straight sets — heaviest pulling movement of the day

exercises.name: Single-arm DB row
exercises.tip: Brace on bench. Let the shoulder blade fully protract at the bottom, retract hard at the top. No torso rotation.

exercises.name: DB rear delt fly (prone on bench)
exercises.tip: Face down on incline bench. Lead with elbows, squeeze shoulder blades together at top.

exercises.name: Resistance band pull-apart
exercises.tip: Arms straight, pull to full stretch. Slow controlled return — don't let it snap back.

exercises.name: Resistance band pulldown
exercises.tip: Drive elbows down and back. Squeeze lats at the bottom of each rep.

exercises.name: Incline DB curl
exercises.tip: Arms hanging on incline bench — full bicep stretch at the bottom. No swinging.

exercises.name: DB hammer curl
exercises.tip: Neutral grip. Slow on the way down — 3 seconds. Hits brachialis for arm thickness.

exercises.name: Farmer carry
exercises.tip: Stand tall, shoulders packed back and down. Don't let the weight pull you sideways.

active.progression: On rows, once 3 × 10 per side feels controlled with no momentum, add weight. On carries, increase distance or weight when 40 steps feels easy.

active.name: Squat Day

blocks.title: Block 1 — Goblet squat ladder (straight sets)
blocks.note: 3 straight sets ascending weight — pattern first, then load

exercises.name: Tempo goblet squat
exercises.note: 3 sec down, 1 sec pause at bottom, drive up
exercises.tip: Elbows inside knees at the bottom. Drive through the full foot on the way up. The tempo is the work — don't rush it.

blocks.title: Block 2 — Single-leg circuit
blocks.note: 2 rounds — unilateral then bilateral to give stabilizers a breather

exercises.name: Bulgarian split squat
exercises.note: Start unweighted, add 15 lb DBs when form is solid
exercises.tip: Rear foot on bench. Front shin stays vertical. Lower slowly — 3 seconds down.

exercises.name: Side step lunge
exercises.tip: Big lateral step, push hips back as you sink. Keep stepping foot fully flat. Bilateral — gives legs a break from balance demand.

blocks.title: Block 3 — Lunge & core circuit
blocks.note: 2 rounds — fresher legs now, earn the bird-dogs

exercises.name: Medicine ball lunge
exercises.tip: Hold med ball at chest. Step forward, lower back knee toward floor. Drive through front heel to return.

exercises.name: Bird-dog
exercises.tip: On all fours. Opposite arm and leg. Keep hips level — don't rotate. Active recovery feel after the lunges.

active.progression: On the goblet squat ladder, add 5 lbs to each set when Set 3 at 50 lbs feels solid. On Bulgarians, add weight when 8 reps per leg is clean and balanced.

active.name: Hinge Day

exercises.name: Single-leg DB RDL
exercises.note: Challenging weight — DBs in both hands
exercises.tip: Soft bend in standing knee. Hinge until you feel a deep hamstring pull. Don't chase depth with a rounded back — the stretch is the goal, not the range.

exercises.name: DB hip thrust (bench)
exercises.note: DB or heavy bag across hips
exercises.tip: Upper back on bench. Drive through heels. Full glute squeeze — hold 1 second at lockout.

exercises.name: Medicine ball slam
exercises.tip: Full extension overhead, violent exhale on the slam. Hinge to pick it up — don't round your back on the retrieve.

exercises.name: Resistance band hip side step
exercises.note: Band around ankles or just above knees
exercises.tip: Stay in a slight squat throughout. Keep tension in the band — don't let feet come together.

exercises.name: Bird-dog
exercises.tip: Opposite arm and leg. Hips stay level — imagine a glass of water on your lower back.

exercises.name: Plate circles
exercises.tip: Arms extended at shoulder height. Slow deliberate circles — shoulder and core stability work.

exercises.name: Single-leg calf raise
exercises.tip: Full range — don't bounce at the bottom. Use a step edge if available.

active.progression: Single-leg RDL is the anchor — once 3 × 8 per leg is controlled and balanced, add weight. On hip thrusts, add load as soon as you can maintain the 1-second lockout hold.

archived.name: Quick Legs

blocks.title: Warm-up flow
blocks.note: Single circuit, done once as a warm-up

exercises.name: Jumping jacks
exercises.tip: Easy pace. Get the blood moving and joints warm before loading anything.

exercises.name: Bodyweight goblet squat
exercises.tip: Hands clasped at chest. Full depth, controlled. Groove the pattern before adding weight.

blocks.title: Main circuit
blocks.rest: Rest 60s at end of each round
blocks.note: 2 rounds — keep rest between exercises short, rest fully between rounds

exercises.name: Weighted calf raise
exercises.note: Hold 25–30 lb DB in each hand
exercises.tip: Stand tall, full range of motion. Don't bounce at the bottom — pause briefly, then drive up. Drop reps and go heavier as you get stronger.

exercises.name: Side-lying hip abduction
exercises.note: Or clam shells with resistance band
exercises.tip: Keep hips stacked, don't roll back. Slow controlled lift — feel the glute med working. Band adds meaningful resistance to clam shells.

exercises.name: Sumo squat
exercises.note: Bodyweight or light DB held at chest
exercises.tip: Wide stance, toes turned out 45°. Sit straight down between your heels. Targets inner thighs and glutes differently than a goblet squat.

exercises.name: Side step squat
exercises.tip: Step out into a squat, bring feet together, repeat. Keep hips low throughout — don't stand up between steps.

exercises.name: Medicine ball lunge
exercises.tip: Hold med ball at chest. Alternate legs. Drive through front heel to return. Keep torso tall.

exercises.name: Glute bridge
exercises.note: Load with DB or heavy bag across hips
exercises.tip: Feet flat, drive through heels. Squeeze glutes hard at the top and hold 1 second. Don't hyperextend your lower back.

exercises.name: Hip rotation stretch
exercises.tip: Figure-four position on your back, or standing hip circles. Breathe into each rep. Let the hips open up after all that loading.

archived.progression: This is a conditioning and volume session — progression is adding load to calf raises and glute bridges first. When 2 rounds feels comfortable, add a third round to the main circuit.

archived.name: Core / Back / PT

blocks.title: Block 1 — Anti-rotation & stability
blocks.rest: Minimal rest between exercises, 60s between rounds
blocks.note: 2 rounds — slow and deliberate, this is nervous system work

exercises.name: Resistance band arm-extended hold
exercises.tip: Band anchored at chest height. Extend arms straight out, rotate away from anchor. The goal is to resist — don't let the band pull you. Core stays braced throughout.

exercises.name: Bird-dog
exercises.tip: On all fours. Opposite arm and leg, extend slowly. Hold at the top — don't rush the hold. Hips stay perfectly level. Quality over quantity here.

exercises.name: Dead bug
exercises.tip: Lie on back, arms up, knees at 90°. Lower opposite arm and leg toward floor without letting your lower back lift. Exhale on the way down. This is harder than it looks.

exercises.name: Med ball wall throw
exercises.tip: Load through the hips and core, not the arms. Throw from rotation, catch and control the rebound. Stand far enough that you're generating real power.

exercises.name: Clam shell side plank
exercises.tip: Side plank position, then open top knee against the band. Keep hips stacked and high — don't let them sag. Glute med and obliques working together.

exercises.name: Boat pose hold
exercises.tip: Seated, lean back, legs lifted, arms extended forward. Keep chest tall — don't collapse through the lower back. Breathe steadily. This earns the flow.

blocks.title: Block 3 — Vinyasa flow cool-down
blocks.rest: No rest — move with your breath

exercises.name: Vinyasa flow
exercises.note: Down dog → cobra → up dog → child's pose
exercises.tip: Move with your breath — inhale to extend, exhale to fold. Don't rush. This is recovery, not performance. Let the spine decompress after all the core work.

archived.progression: This session is about quality of movement, not load. Progression means longer holds, better form, and more control — not heavier weights. Add a third round to Blocks 1 and 2 when 2 rounds feels easy.
